title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers in Brookline vote to authorize a strike amid stalled contract negotiations.
Union spokesperson says strike not immediate, citing need for more negotiation on job protection and self-service kiosks.
Executive Director Beth Gilligan says theatre remains in good-faith negotiations, presenting a proposal without imminent strike plans.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 season.
FanGraphs projects no Red Sox player to hit 20 or more home runs in 2026, contrasting Cora's expectations.
Abreu hit 22 home runs in 2025 and impressed in the 2026 World Baseball Classic with Team Venezuela.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots may draft Arizona State offensive tackle Max Iheanachor with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Iheanachor, a 6-foot-6, 321-pound tackle, earned Second Team All-Big 12 honors as a two-year starter.
NFL experts view Iheanachor as a promising player with potential to develop into a high-level starter for New England.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials condemned racist comments following the South High vs. Andover High girls basketball game on March 6.
The comments were spurred by a viral video showing a South High player involved in a push during the game.
The NAACP and YWCA criticized the remarks, citing harmful stereotypes and the need to protect student-athletes.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, from Rehoboth, faces charges for allegedly sexually exploiting four girls aged 12 to 16.
Authorities found child sexual abuse material on Mota's phone at Logan International Airport during a June 2025 search.
Mota admitted receiving hundreds of illicit images and videos from minors, paying them via Apple Cash and Cash App.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection reopened Thursday after a burst water main flooded Jamaica Plain streets, disrupting traffic for two days.
The flooding, initially attributed to a 36-inch main, was later linked to a ruptured 12-inch pipe feeding into the main.
The 1897 cast iron pipe replacement and road restoration completed Thursday, after 48 hours of continuous repair efforts.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Dr. Derrick Todd, 52, indicted on three rape and 17 indecent assault counts, tied to 11 patients in Framingham.
Todd allegedly performed unnecessary pelvic and breast exams between 2021 and 2023 at Charles River Medical Associates.
He was a Brigham and Women’s physician until 2023; more than 200 patients joined a class action lawsuit against him.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul advises using a second lithium iron phosphate battery for powering appliances in a van conversion.
A pure sine wave converter is recommended for clean power when running electronics during van life.
Rooftop solar panels and vehicle alternators can charge the auxiliary battery in a converted camper van.

title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Charges against former Brockton choral director Matthew Cunningham were dropped after the alleged victim declined to testify.
Cunningham was initially charged with trafficking and sexual conduct after an investigation flagged a payment to a minor.
Brockton Public Schools placed Cunningham on paid administrative leave; he is no longer employed there.

title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old boy were arrested for armed robbery of an elderly woman in Charlestown.
The suspects allegedly pointed a gun at the woman and stole her purse containing credit cards, police said.
Jordan was charged with armed robbery and other offenses, and is being held without bail pending a hearing.
